item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Two proposals here. First, this article is mentioned on the MTF hub as being associated with MTF Omega-0, so a link there is not out of line. Additionally, the character Marrion Wheeler has made many appearances throughout the wiki, most notable of which is Case Colourless Green. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

A monotone voice is perceived, which states "You are not an MT—[indiscernable] agent. Access denied." Further testing on this command is forbidden.

To this:

A monotone voice is perceived, which states "You are not an MT—[indiscernable] agent. Access denied." Further testing on this command is forbidden.

Editing this:

All members of Research Team 3664 must receive training on managing their thoughts from the Memetics Division prior to being exposed to knowledge of SCP-3664. Persons prone to daydreaming or similar behaviors will not be allowed on the team. As per orders by Senior Researcher Marion Wheeler, Testing Chamber 3664 is the only location where detailed information on the weapon is permitted. Persons wholly compromised by cognitohazardous phenomena produced by SCP-3664 will be put into appropriate detainment.

To this:

All members of Research Team 3664 must receive training on managing their thoughts from the Memetics Division prior to being exposed to knowledge of SCP-3664. Persons prone to daydreaming or similar behaviors will not be allowed on the team. As per orders by Senior Researcher Marion Wheeler, Testing Chamber 3664 is the only location where detailed information on the weapon is permitted. Persons wholly compromised by cognitohazardous phenomena produced by SCP-3664 will be put into appropriate detainment.
Launch the imaginary fireworks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can see the appeal of a deep mystery, but I'm personally happy without one. General hints at what is happening is what I feel works best here. Glad you enjoyed it either way!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing that out! I've changed it to gun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly liked it better before the addendum. The mystery was thick, and ending on having them believe they are part of an MTF on an important mission was a strong end. However, I don't have many qualms with the last addendum, even if I think it would have been stronger without it. +1. Love concept rifle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting!
One nitpick: I'm pretty sure ASL doesn't have a specific sign for the word "weapon". Some words used in English for broad categories of things, like the word "tool" for instance, don't really have a single corresponding sign in ASL. Typically, to communicate that concept you'd probably finger spell it, then sign a list of examples or what they do. (Eg: "W-E-A-P-O-N gun, shoot…knife, stab…bomb, explode" or something like that).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotcha (I've already made the date change ftr).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Probably, yes, on both counts.

I will let this percolate and see what comes up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll just pretend that some people have tried but it didn't stop (or the sound didn't bother anyone) so I can act like I realized that was something that could be done. >_<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems like a great spot for some drama potential! :) Also, why has no one tried disabling the tracking beacon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though I do wonder whether the description would enable anyone to use the thing after reading the article.

That's exactly what would happen in-universe, at least if they just read the info on its appearance. The full file with said information is only given to the subjects in testing.

Have they tried dual-subject testing?

If you mean like multiple people using the anomaly at the same time, that wasn't something I'd actually thought. If I were to take an educated guess it probably has happened, and it definitely was a chaotic mess (lotsa plasma and shenanigans).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is intriguing. Though I do wonder whether the description would enable anyone to use the thing after reading the article. Have they tried dual-subject testing? Also I hate the word 'thrum'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would setting it to like 2014 work then? Also note that I imagine that more versions of this weapon are currently in use so having someone use this at a later date shouldn't be a big deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to qtnm, Site-41 and Marion Wheeler were lost in 2015.

I think that I have already played a little loosey goosey with some of the characters that I am using relative to this though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, I could adjust the dates to something that fits better (don't know what year your series is set in). I'm not attached to the current year choice of 2020 anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will think about this. It is definitely stretching what should be possible.

On the other hand, having this appear in "What the Dead Know" is tempting. The timeline doesn't work, unfortunately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That actually makes a lot of sense, though there's no real consensus on how things moving between reality and the noosphere works (having the cadaver be less real than imagined does skirt around this issue so I guess it checks out).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took it as the gun’s affect bringing him into reality where he could be killed, so I don’t think them normally not being able to do that would violate any continuity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Sorta spoilers I guess?
UNSPOILING IS FUTILE

That's pretty much what went down, though I won't word-of-god wholly confirm this MTF to be Omega-Zero without sirpudding's blessings (mostly because I'm not sure if it'd be possible for Omega-Zero agent to push themselves back into physical reality, unless the corpse is actually just a memetic construct). If no blessings the other possibility is that this is another MTF that is bouncing themselves in and out of the noosphere, unaware of the existence of Omega-Zero (as most people are).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I understand this right, this is a weapon developed for omega zero that somehow got into the hands of the normal foundation who can’t perceive it properly? And the guy appearing at the end was an ara orun operative trying to get it back while being followed by 3125?

item.title: I thought about upvoting this article.
item.content:encoded: And then it was. Strange.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never watched Dragonball but I can see a mild resemblance between the main plasma shots of SCP-3664 and this, soooooo maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So is this like the special beam cannon from Dragonball?

If so, +1

item.title: Try not to misfire
item.content:encoded:
Might as well get this posted now before Doomcon comes along like a tsunami of posts.

Thanks to MaliceAforethought, Phantom Ink, Drblackbox, GibberingEloquence, Mortos, Captain Kirby, PeppersGhost, whomstdveWorm , DrChandra, Weryllium for reading and critting!
